decision_rule.fern_357_rule_verbatim: relax is justified iff (TPS gain over deployed >= human-set threshold) AND (measured PPL <= 2.42) AND (the break is the SAME KIND already deployed — accumulation-order flips, not a new failure mode)

ledger.ppl_gate_margin_note: >= 0.0428 — identity 1.0 => byte-exact greedy reference stream => PPL is the reference quality the gate (reference + 5%) is defined against; admissible by construction. Not separately measured this cycle

UNMEASURED — stark #452 quality run to fill. Available PPL budget from the deployed anchor = 0.0428 (2.42 - 2.3772). PPL-admissible IFF measured PPL <= 2.42. Flip-count tells you NOTHING about this — flips can be PPL-neutral (near-ties) OR PPL-breaching

ppl_ledger.orthogonality_note: flip-COUNT and PPL are DIFFERENT, ORTHOGONAL costs. A flip is a token-ID divergence from the greedy reference (an identity/equivalence cost); PPL is a quality cost. Flips can be PPL-NEUTRAL (reduction-order near-ties, like the deployed 3) or PPL-BREACHING (genuine quality loss). You CANNOT infer PPL-admissibility from the flip count — that conflation is the trap that sank four modeled-in-isolation levers. The ledger keeps the two in separate columns and never derives one from the other
ppl_ledger.summary: gate PPL <= 2.42; deployed PPL 2.3772 => margin 0.0428. strict = reference-quality (admissible by construction). relax PPL UNMEASURED — admissible only if a quality run (stark #452) reads <= 2.42
